---
title: "Lectures"
description: Computational Neuroscience
output:
  html_document:
    toc: false 
    theme: cosmo
fontsize: 14pt
---

- Week 1
    - <a href="lectures/week_1/lecture_variable_assignment.html" target="_blank">Variable Assignment and Data Types in Python</a>
    - <a href="lectures/week_1/lecture_container_types.html" target="_blank">Container Variable Types in Python and Accessing Elements</a>
    - <a href="lectures/week_1/lecture_functions.html" target="_blank">Creating and Calling Functions in Python</a>
    - <a href="lectures/week_1/lecture_type_coercion.html" target="_blank">Type Coercion in Python</a>
    - <a href="lectures/week_1/lecture_loops_cond_flow.html" target="_blank">Loops and Conditional Flow in Python</a>
    - <a href="lectures/week_1/lecture_getting_help.html" target="_blank">Getting Help with Python Programming</a>
    - <a href="lectures/week_1/lecture_mre.html" target="_blank">Creating a Minimal Reproducible Example (MRE)</a>

- Week 2
    - <a href="lectures/week_2/lecture_array_basics.html" target="_blank">NumPy Array Basics</a>
    - <a href="lectures/week_2/lecture_creating_arrays.html" target="_blank">Creating Numpy Arrays</a>
    - <a href="lectures/week_2/lecture_indexing_slicing.html" target="_blank">Indexing and Slicing in NumPy</a>
    - <a href="lectures/week_2/lecture_element_vs_aggregate.html" target="_blank">Element-wise vs Aggregate Operations in NumPy</a>
    - <a href="lectures/week_2/lecture_broadcasting.html" target="_blank">Broadcasting in NumPy</a>
    - <a href="lectures/week_2/lecture_reshape_transpose.html" target="_blank">Reshaping and Transposing Arrays</a>
    - <a href="lectures/week_2/lecture_basic_stats.html" target="_blank">Basic Statistics with NumPy</a>
